summary: Notion AI is aggressively expanding its global sales and enterprise GTM capabilities, with targeted hiring across EMEA, Asia (especially Japan/Tokyo), and AMER. Simultaneously, it is strengthening AI-specific security infrastructure and core platform engineering—particularly in datastores, data platforms, and cloud infrastructure—to support scaling and trust for enterprise adoption.

signals.title: Outbound Business Development Representative (x2)
signals.insight: Dual postings signal accelerated outbound motion to acquire new logos, especially among SMBs and mid-market, intensifying competition for early-stage workflow-AI buyers.

signals.title: Sales Manager, Asia & Sales Development Representative, Japan
signals.insight: Dedicated Japan and broader Asia sales leadership roles indicate a strategic push into high-growth, underpenetrated markets where competitors like ClickUp and Coda have limited localized GTM—creating first-mover advantage in enterprise AI collaboration.

signals.title: Solutions Engineer, EMEA & Solutions Engineer, Tokyo
signals.insight: Regional solutions engineering hires suggest imminent expansion of technical sales capacity to support complex enterprise evaluations—directly challenging Microsoft Loop and Asana's AI offerings in regulated, high-touch markets.

signals.title: Application Security Engineer, AI Security
signals.insight: First dedicated AI Security role signals formalization of AI-specific threat modeling and red-teaming—critical for winning regulated enterprise contracts where AI governance (e.g., SOC 2 AI addendum) is now table stakes.

signals.title: Software Engineer, Datastore & Data Platform
signals.insight: Concurrent hiring for datastore and data platform engineers reveals foundational investment in scalable, low-latency AI data infrastructure—enabling real-time RAG, personalization, and offline sync critical for competing with Figma's and Linear's embedded AI performance.

signals.title: Enterprise Customer Success Manager, AMER & Manager, Scaled Customer Success
signals.insight: Dual-tier customer success hiring reflects maturation toward enterprise land-and-expand motions, directly threatening Atlassian's Confluence AI adoption by improving retention and cross-sell velocity in large accounts.

signals.title: Enterprise Product Marketing, GTM
signals.insight: Dedicated enterprise PMM signals upcoming launch of differentiated AI packaging (e.g., AI governance dashboards, usage analytics, or compliance modules) aimed squarely at displacing legacy intranet and knowledge management tools.

signals.title: Enterprise Sales Leader
signals.insight: Hiring a top-tier enterprise sales leader—rather than promoting internally—indicates urgency to close $1M+ ACV deals and signals readiness to compete head-to-head with Microsoft's Copilot for M365 in Fortune 500 procurement cycles.
